title: coleus marrubatus
intro: Coleus marrubatus, also known as Bitter Leaf or Horehound, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bitter-tasting leaves and purple flowers. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its expectorant properties, which help relieve coughs and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Coleus marrubatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and even act as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Coleus marrubatus are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
